name: Job Search Pipeline
description: Automated job listing scraper, AI-powered relevance scorer, and ranked shortlist generator. Scrapes listings via python-jobspy, classifies seniority and domain fit with Haiku Batch, scores shortlisted roles against a target profile with Sonnet, and delivers a ranked digest.

defaultStack.rationale: Multi-board scraper (LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor, ZipRecruiter) with a single interface. Handles pagination and rate limiting. No Playwright needed unless jobspy coverage is insufficient.
Used only for boards not covered by jobspy. Always async — never sync Playwright in production code.
Classifies seniority (junior/mid/senior/principal/director), domain fit, and remote eligibility across all scraped listings. 50% batch discount. ~$0.06/day at 500 listings.
Generates structured match score against target profile for Tier A survivors only. Hard cap: 20 listings per run. No listing enters Tier B without passing seniority filter.
Deduplicates listings across scrape runs by embedding title+company+location. Cosine similarity > 0.92 suppresses re-processing of seen listings.
Sufficient for <100k listings. Stores raw JD text, Tier A labels, Tier B scores, and digest history.
Daily DAG: scrape → deduplicate → Tier A classify → Tier B score → digest. Separate DAGs for scraping and scoring allow independent retries.

phaseHints: Phase 1 — Scraping & dedup: jobspy scraper DAG, SQLite listings table, ChromaDB semantic dedup, seen_listings fingerprint table, --dry-run mode
Phase 2 — Tier A classification: Haiku Batch job on unseen listings, seniority + domain + remote labels, confidence scores, filter pipeline (discard junior/mid)
Phase 3 — Tier B scoring: Sonnet structured match score against target profile Pydantic model, hard 20-listing cap, ranked output table, digest generation
Phase 4 — Delivery & reporting: daily digest email or Slack message, score trend charts, Tier B spend tracker, threshold tuning based on digest quality

architectureDecisions.choice: No listing enters Tier B unless Haiku labels it principal, architect, staff, or director with confidence > 0.75
architectureDecisions.rationale: Empirically eliminates ~75% of listings from Sonnet spend. Confidence threshold is tunable; start at 0.75 and adjust based on false negative rate in digest review.

architectureDecisions.choice: Two-layer: exact fingerprint (title+company+location hash) for same-day dedup, ChromaDB semantic similarity for cross-day dedup
architectureDecisions.rationale: Exact hash is fast and cheap. Semantic dedup catches reposted listings with minor title variations (e.g. 'Senior Engineer' vs 'Sr. Engineer, Platform').

architectureDecisions.choice: Plain text only in SQLite — no raw HTML
architectureDecisions.rationale: HTML inflates token count by 3-5× when sent to LLMs. Extract text at ingest time. Store HTML separately only if rendering is needed.

costModelHint: At 500 listings/day: Haiku Batch classification ~$0.06/day. Sonnet (20 listings × ~2k tokens I/O) ~$0.09/day. ChromaDB embedding (500 × 200 tokens) ~$0.002/day. Total: ~$0.15/day, ~$4.50/month. Hard cap on Tier B enforced in scorer/tier_b.py — log warning and truncate if input exceeds 20 listings.
